title: Frobenius Structures on Quantum Connections of Calabi--Yau 3-Folds
speaker: Jae Hee Lee
abstract: The quantum connection is a flat connection arising from genus 0 Gromov--Witten theory. They can be defined integrally for sufficiently positive symplectic manifolds, allowing one to consider their characteristic p or p-adic versions which bear similarity to Gauss--Manin connections in arithmetic geometry. We focus on the case of Calabi--Yau 3-folds and explain the arithmetic aspects of the quantum connection, emphasizing the role of quantum power operations. This talk is based on joint work with Shaoyun Bai and Daniel Pomerleano.

proof_stages.label: Quantum cohomology and the flat quantum connection
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines small quantum cohomology over a Novikov ring, packages quantum multiplication by degree-two classes into logarithmic quantum connection operators, and recalls their flatness and the role of the equivariant variable u.

proof_stages.label: Arithmetic integral form of quantum connections
proof_stages.summary: Using symplectic definitions of genus-zero Gromov-Witten counts, the speaker explains when quantum connections can be defined over integers, finite fields, or p-adic integers, and motivates the search for arithmetic structures by mirror symmetry with Gauss-Manin connections.

proof_stages.label: p-curvature for characteristic-p connections
proof_stages.summary: For connections over F_p, the p-th iterate of a derivation is again a derivation, giving a tensorial p-curvature that measures failure of the connection to preserve the p-power operation and obstructs formal flat sections.

proof_stages.label: Quantum Steenrod operations
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls classical Steenrod operations as equivariant Frobenius-type operations and introduces their quantum deformation by counting Z/p-equivariant genus-zero curves with p cyclically placed insertions.

proof_stages.label: Characteristic-p theorem: p-curvature equals quantum Steenrod
proof_stages.summary: For a Calabi-Yau threefold and an integral degree-two cohomology class, the p-curvature of the corresponding quantum connection operator is identified with the associated quantum Steenrod operation, giving both a computation of the latter and an enumerative interpretation of the former.

proof_stages.label: p-adic Frobenius lift and horizontal intertwiner
proof_stages.summary: Over Z_p, the Frobenius lift q ↦ q^p is used to formulate a rare horizontality problem, and the second theorem asserts the existence of a Frobenius-twisted horizontal map with strong p-adic divisibility respecting cohomological degree.

proof_stages.label: Inverse Cartier operator and comparison diagram
proof_stages.summary: Dividing the p-adic Frobenius intertwiner by the predicted powers of p and reducing modulo p produces an inverse Cartier operator, which is shown to relate Frobenius-twisted quantum multiplication to p-curvature and conceptually explains the characteristic-p theorem as a shadow of the p-adic structure.

proof_stages.label: Calabi-Yau threefold reduction by embedded-curve structure
proof_stages.summary: The proof uses the special genus-zero structure theorem for Calabi-Yau threefolds: Gromov-Witten theory is controlled by integer counts of embedded rational-curve local geometries, so the stated operations and comparison theorems reduce to computations in that local model and then extend by integer linear combination.

examples.name: The equation f' = f in characteristic p
examples.note: The formal exponential equation illustrates how nonzero p-curvature obstructs formal solvability in characteristic p, after p-curvature has already been defined.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker uses f'=f to explain formal solvability in characteristic zero versus characteristic p and relates the operator ∂z−1 and its p-th power to nonzero p-curvature

passes.reason: The equation f'=f in characteristic p is used to show formal solvability can fail because its p-curvature is nonzero.
